title: Promotion Strategy Guidance
text: When appointment-to-order conversion declines while appointment volume remains stable, consider targeted manager follow-up, offer clarity, and appointment reminder improvements before increasing discount depth.

title: Store Operations Playbook
text: A simultaneous drop in sales orders and POS invoices may indicate store traffic weakness, checkout system latency, or promotion-message mismatch. Compare with appointment show rate and work order backlog.

title: Work Order Backlog Guidance
text: High average work order cycle time can reduce same-day completion capacity and may lower customer conversion. Prioritize overdue work orders and verify staffing coverage.

title: Executive KPI Narrative Pattern
text: Executives usually need region-level variance, affected stores, top contributing metrics, likely causes, and recommended strategic actions with expected business impact.

title: Inventory Health Response Guide
text: When in-stock rate drops below 90% or stockout SKU count exceeds 3, expedite replenishment orders, pause promotions on constrained SKUs, redirect demand to healthy substitutes, and notify affected customers proactively.

title: Branded Revenue Mix Strategy
text: Low branded revenue mix rate signals missed margin expansion opportunities. Deploy branded upsell scripts at POS checkout, feature premium products in appointment consultations, and coach advisors on value-based selling.

title: Conversion Funnel Diagnostic
text: The conversion funnel spans appointment show → order creation → POS capture. Weakness at any stage compounds downstream. Diagnose each stage independently before applying blanket promotions.

title: Refund Rate Investigation Protocol
text: Refund rate above 8% warrants investigation into product defects, service mismatches, and return policy abuse. Cross-reference with work order cycle time and appointment-to-order conversion to isolate root causes.
